claims.claim: John Jumper announced on June 19, 2026 that he is leaving Google DeepMind to join Anthropic after nearly nine years.
Jumper said he will take time to recharge before starting, and neither Anthropic nor Jumper disclosed his role or title.
The 2024 Nobel Prize in Chemistry awarded one half to David Baker and the other half jointly to Demis Hassabis and John Jumper for protein structure prediction.
As of November 2025 the AlphaFold Protein Structure Database had been used by over 3 million researchers in more than 190 countries and holds over 200 million predicted structures.
Noam Shazeer, Google VP of engineering and a Gemini co-lead, announced on June 18, 2026 that he is leaving Google for OpenAI, one day before Jumper's announcement.
Anthropic launched Claude for Life Sciences on October 20, 2025, built on Claude Sonnet 4.5 with connectors to Benchling, 10x Genomics, PubMed and others.
On February 2, 2026 Anthropic named the Allen Institute and Howard Hughes Medical Institute (Janelia) as founding life-sciences partners.
Anthropic's AI for Science Program, launched May 5, 2025, offers researchers up to $20,000 in API credits over six months.
Dario Amodei's October 2024 essay argues AI could compress 50-100 years of biological progress into 5-10 years, a 'country of geniuses in a datacenter'.
Anthropic activated its ASL-3 protections with Claude Opus 4 in May 2025, specifically to limit chemical and biological weapons misuse.
Anthropic raised a $65B Series H at a $965B post-money valuation, announced May 28, 2026.
Anthropic raised a $30B Series G at a $380B post-money valuation, announced February 12, 2026.
Anthropic raised a $13B Series F at a $183B post-money valuation, announced September 2, 2025.
Claude Code's run-rate revenue exceeded $2.5B as of February 2026, the dominant driver of Anthropic's growth.
Alphabet's FY2025 research and development expense was $61.087B, up from $49.326B in FY2024.
Alphabet guided 2026 capital expenditure to $175-185B, up from $91.4B in 2025.
Isomorphic Labs signed drug-discovery deals with Eli Lilly and Novartis in January 2024 worth nearly $3B in potential milestones.
Isomorphic Labs raised a $600M external round in March 2025 and reportedly a $2.1B Series B in May 2026.
Isomorphic Labs delayed its first-in-human clinical trials from end-2025 to end-2026.
AlphaFold3 achieved a 58% ligand-binding success rate versus 24% for the docking tool AutoDock Vina.
AlphaFold2 was trained on a Protein Data Bank snapshot from August 28, 2019 containing roughly 170,000 experimental structures.
At CASP14 in 2020 AlphaFold2 reached a median GDT score of 92.4, versus 58.9 for AlphaFold1 at CASP13 in 2018.
SignalFire's 2025 report found DeepMind engineers were nearly 11 times more likely to leave for Anthropic than the reverse, and Anthropic led labs on two-year retention at 80%.
Andrej Karpathy, an OpenAI co-founder, joined Anthropic's pre-training team on May 19, 2026.
Bloomberg reported Jumper had been a key member of Google's coding-tools team, work the company struggled to sell to businesses.
Anthropic acquired Coefficient Bio, an eight-month-old computational-biology startup with fewer than 10 staff, for about $400M in stock in April 2026.
On BioMysteryBench, Claude Opus 4.6 scored 77.4% on human-solvable bioinformatics tasks but only 23.5% on human-difficult ones.
Anthropic scheduled a virtual 'Briefing: AI for Science' event for June 30, 2026 with guests from Novartis, BMS and Genentech; Jumper is not on the roster.
Alphabet stock did not sell off on the departures; GOOGL rose about 1.17% on June 19, 2026 and ended the week up roughly 2.3%.
DeepMind's science portfolio extends well beyond AlphaFold, including GNoME (2.2 million predicted crystals), AlphaGenome, AlphaProteo and weather forecasting.
A systematic review by physicist Nick McGreivy found that 79% of surveyed AI-for-science papers used weak baselines, inflating apparent gains.
The US DOE Genesis Mission committed over $320M to AI-for-science in December 2025 and named Anthropic, OpenAI and Google DeepMind as partners; NIH Bridge2AI invested $130M.
AlphaFold2 was published in Nature in July 2021 and AlphaFold3, co-developed with Isomorphic Labs, in May 2024.
Jumper holds a PhD in theoretical chemistry from the University of Chicago (2017) and previously worked at D. E. Shaw Research; he led AlphaFold within months of finishing his PhD.

Estimates of the AI drug-discovery software market cluster around $1-3B today, while AI across all life sciences is put at roughly $13-21B and McKinsey estimates $60-110B in annual potential economic value.
No AI-designed drug has yet received FDA approval, and 2025 saw shutdowns and layoffs among AI drug-discovery startups.
David Baker, the 2024 Nobel co-laureate, launched the drug-design startup Xaira Therapeutics with around $1B in funding in 2024.
